mechanism_narrative: A sustained federal funds rate below 0.25% compresses equity discount rates and lowers borrowing costs for corporations, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive when FEDFUNDS < 0.25% (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 9.0% indicates severe labor market deterioration that suppresses household consumption and corporate revenue growth, systematically producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in real retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand recovery, reducing economic uncertainty and driving positive equity market returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 20d returns in > 55% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-crisis recovery environment, a month-over-month increase in the industrial production index reflects improving factory utilization and capacity expansion, generating positive short-term equity momentum over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5d returns during recovery phases with accuracy below 52%, or signal reverses direction.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPIAUCSL index remains below 215.0 while policy rates stay at the zero lower bound, deeply negative real interest rates stimulate risk asset allocation and liquidity-driven equity buying, producing positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Returns are negative or flat when CPIAUCSL < 215.0 in > 65% of observations, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payrolls indicates persistent economic weakness and reduced consumer spending power, systematically depressing corporate earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS declines are followed by positive 60d returns in > 58% of cases, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery, quarter-over-quarter GDP growth exceeding 0.5% confirms economic stabilization and reduces tail-risk premiums, supporting positive equity market returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth > 0.5% fails to predict positive 20d returns during recovery periods with accuracy below 53%, or signal accuracy drops below chance.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between industrial production and retail sales, where factory output rises while consumer spending stagnates, signals inventory buildup and impending demand weakness, triggering negative short-term equity returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases combined with RSAFS declines do not precede negative 5d returns in > 55% of cases, or interaction effect is statistically insignificant.
